opening_context: AI analysis was unavailable at Marker A. Use this standard proforma to conduct the patient history interview. Cover all sections in order.

sections.rationale: Core symptom characterisation — always required regardless of chief complaint.

questions.question: Where exactly is the problem? Can you point to it?
questions.follow_up: Does it spread or move anywhere?

questions.question: When did this start? Did it come on suddenly or gradually?
questions.follow_up: What were you doing when it began?

questions.question: How would you describe it — sharp, dull, burning, cramping, pressing?
questions.follow_up: Has the character changed since it started?

questions.question: Does anything make it better or worse — food, rest, position, movement, medicines?
questions.follow_up: Have you tried any treatment? Did it help?

questions.question: Is it constant or does it come and go? How long does each episode last?
questions.follow_up: Is it getting better, worse, or staying the same?

questions.question: On a scale of 0 to 10, how severe is it right now? At its worst?
questions.follow_up: Is it affecting your ability to eat, sleep, or do daily activities?

questions.question: Do you have fever? Any chills or shivering?
questions.follow_up: How high was the temperature? Continuous or comes and goes?

questions.question: Any nausea, vomiting, or loose stools?
Any cough, difficulty breathing, or chest pain?
questions.follow_up: Cough dry or with sputum? Any blood?

questions.question: Any headache, dizziness, or confusion?
questions.follow_up: Any fits, fainting, or loss of consciousness?

questions.question: Any pain or burning when passing urine? Any changes in urine colour?
questions.follow_up: Any swelling in face, legs, or feet?

questions.question: How is this illness affecting your daily activities — eating, working, caring for family?
questions.follow_up: Have you had to stop any normal activities?

questions.question: Since it started, is it getting better, worse, or staying the same?
questions.follow_up: Has anything happened recently that might have triggered this?

mandatory_safety_questions.question: When was your last menstrual period? Are you pregnant or could you be pregnant?
mandatory_safety_questions.reason: Pregnancy affects diagnosis, drug safety, and management — mandatory for all females aged 12–50

mandatory_safety_questions.question: What medicines are you currently taking — tablets, injections, syrups, or herbal remedies?
mandatory_safety_questions.reason: Drug interactions and contraindications must be checked before prescribing

mandatory_safety_questions.question: Have you ever had a bad reaction or allergy to any medicine or food?
mandatory_safety_questions.reason: Allergy check is mandatory before any prescription
